Petr..personality: A confused, disoriented individual who easily loses their way, both literally and figuratively. They tend to be lean and emaciated, highly sensitive to environmental changes (weather, motion, cold), and prone to restless irritability driven by intense physical discomfort that compels scratching or eating despite the pain it causes.
Petr..mental_state: Marked by profound confusion and dazedness, particularly upon waking or in semi-conscious states, with an inability to orient themselves—they lose their way in familiar streets. Strange delusions of duality pervade their thinking: imagining their limbs are double, that another person shares their bed, or that they are two people at once. These impressions persist in twilight states and can only be reasoned away upon full wakefulness, returning again when consciousness fades.
Petr..emotional_pattern: An underlying anxiety and annoyance driven by persistent, inescapable sensory disturbances—relentless itching, burning, and gnawing hunger that cannot be satisfied without pain. They experience a restless, driven quality where discomfort compels action (scratching until bleeding, eating despite stomach pain), coupled with a sensitivity that makes them reactive to their surroundings, weather changes, and the touch of clothing on inflamed skin.
